question: What does the open jar symbol on a skincare product tell you?
options: The product should be stored in a jar
How many months to use the product after opening it
The product contains natural ingredients
The product is vegan and cruelty-free

question: What does PAO stand for on skincare packaging?
options: Product Absorption Order
Period After Opening which tells you how long to use a product once opened
Pure Active Oil
Premium Antioxidant Output

question: What does a 12M on the open jar symbol mean?
options: The product was made 12 months ago
Use the product within 12 months of opening
The product contains 12 active ingredients
The product lasts 12 years unopened

question: What does the term fragrance-free mean on a skincare label?
options: The product smells like flowers naturally
The product has had no fragrance or scent added which is better for sensitive skin
The product is unscented because it expired
The product is a natural organic formula

question: What is the difference between fragrance-free and unscented?
options: They mean exactly the same thing
Fragrance-free contains no fragrance ingredients while unscented may still contain masking fragrances to cover the smell of ingredients
Unscented is always better than fragrance-free
Fragrance-free products always have a natural scent

question: Where on a product label will you find all the ingredients listed?
options: On the front of the packaging in large letters
On the back or bottom of the product under the INCI list
Only available on the brand website
Inside the product box only not on the bottle

question: What does INCI stand for on a skincare label?
options: International Natural Cosmetic Ingredients
International Nomenclature of Cosmetic Ingredients which is the standardised system for listing skincare ingredients
Independent Natural Care Index
International No-Chemical Ingredients

question: Why are ingredients listed in a specific order on skincare products?
options: They are listed alphabetically
Ingredients are listed from highest to lowest concentration so the first ingredients make up most of the product
They are listed by how beneficial they are
They are listed by cost from most to least expensive

question: What does it mean if water or aqua is listed first on an ingredient list?
options: The product contains no active ingredients
Water is the main ingredient and makes up the largest proportion of the formula
The product is designed for very dry skin
The product is a toner or essence type formula

question: What does non-comedogenic mean on a skincare label?
options: The product contains no chemicals
The product will not clog your pores making it suitable for acne-prone skin
The product is certified organic
The product has been tested on sensitive skin

question: What does dermatologist tested mean on a product label?
options: A dermatologist invented the product
A dermatologist has evaluated the product though it does not necessarily mean it was approved or recommended
All ingredients were prescribed by a doctor
The product is only available with a prescription

question: What does hypoallergenic mean on a skincare product?
options: The product contains no ingredients whatsoever
The product is formulated to minimise the risk of allergic reactions though it does not guarantee no reaction
The product is suitable for all skin types with no exceptions
The product has been approved by medical professionals

question: What ingredient on a label indicates a product may be drying or irritating for sensitive skin?
options: Glycerin
Hyaluronic acid
Alcohol denat or SD alcohol listed high on the ingredients
Aloe barbadensis

question: What is the Latin name for aloe vera on an ingredient list?
options: Aloe officinalis
Aloe barbadensis miller
Rosa canina
Calendula officinalis

question: What ingredient should people with nut allergies be cautious of in skincare?
options: Glycerin
Hyaluronic acid
Shea butter or almond oil which come from tree nuts
Niacinamide

question: What does the leaping bunny symbol on skincare packaging mean?
options: The product contains rabbit-derived ingredients
The product is certified cruelty-free and no animal testing was done at any stage
The product is vegan with no animal-derived ingredients
The product was made in an eco-friendly facility

question: What is the difference between cruelty-free and vegan skincare?
options: They mean exactly the same thing
Cruelty-free means no animal testing while vegan means no animal-derived ingredients a product can be one without being the other
Vegan is always a higher certification than cruelty-free
Cruelty-free means the product contains natural ingredients

question: What does the expiry date on a skincare product look like and what does it mean?
options: It is shown as a PAO symbol only
It may appear as a best before date or expiry date after which the product may be less effective or unsafe to use
Skincare products never expire
Expiry dates only apply to food not skincare

question: Is it safe to use skincare products past their expiry date?
options: Yes expired products always work the same
No expired products can lose effectiveness have ingredient breakdown and may harbour bacteria that cause irritation or infection
Only moisturisers are safe to use after expiry
Products are fine to use up to 5 years past expiry

question: What does the green leaf EU organic certification mean on a skincare product?
options: The product is made entirely from plants
At least 95 percent of the agricultural ingredients are organic and it meets EU organic certification standards
The product is environmentally friendly packaging
The product is suitable for vegans

question: What does SPF 30 broad spectrum mean on a sunscreen label?
options: It blocks 30 percent of all UV rays
It filters about 97 percent of UVB rays and broad spectrum means it also protects against UVA rays
It lasts 30 hours without reapplication
It is only suitable for skin tone 30 on the Fitzpatrick scale

question: What should you look for on a sunscreen label to make sure it protects against both types of UV rays?
options: An SPF number above 15
The words broad spectrum which confirms both UVA and UVB protection
The words water-resistant
A high percentage of zinc oxide only

question: What does the term active ingredient mean on a sunscreen or medicated skincare label?
options: The ingredient that gives the product its scent
The ingredient that has been officially recognised to provide the main therapeutic or protective function of the product
The ingredient present in the highest concentration
The ingredient added last in the formula

question: What does paraben-free mean on a skincare label?
options: The product contains no preservatives of any kind
The product does not contain parabens which are a type of preservative some people prefer to avoid
The product is certified organic
The product is suitable for all skin types

question: What are parabens and why do some people try to avoid them?
options: Parabens are a type of fragrance ingredient
Parabens are preservatives that have been controversial due to concerns about potential hormonal disruption though current evidence at normal use levels is debated
Parabens are a natural plant-derived ingredient
Parabens are only found in organic skincare

question: What does sulfate-free mean on a body wash or shampoo label?
options: The product contains no water
The product does not contain sulfates like sodium lauryl sulfate which can be stripping and irritating for sensitive skin
The product has no lather at all
The product is pH balanced

question: What does the recycling symbol on skincare packaging mean?
options: The product ingredients are all recycled
The packaging material can be recycled though you should check local guidelines
The brand donates a portion of sales to recycling programs
The product bottle must be returned to the store

question: What does the term clinically proven mean on a skincare label?
options: It means the product was invented by a doctor
It means clinical studies were conducted though results can vary widely and the quality of studies is not always disclosed
It means the product is a prescription formula
It means all claims have been approved by a government agency

question: What ingredient is commonly listed as tocopherol on skincare labels?
options: Vitamin C
Vitamin D
Vitamin E which is an antioxidant and skin-nourishing ingredient
Vitamin K

question: What does retinyl palmitate refer to on an ingredient list?
options: A synthetic fragrance ingredient
A mild form of vitamin A that is less potent than retinol often used in gentler anti-ageing products
A type of glycerin for hydration
A mineral sunscreen ingredient

question: What is sodium hyaluronate on an ingredient label?
options: A type of salt preservative
A smaller molecule form of hyaluronic acid that can penetrate skin more deeply to hydrate
A synthetic fragrance compound
A vitamin C derivative for brightening

question: What does the ingredient butylene glycol do in skincare formulas?
options: It provides SPF protection
It is a humectant and solvent that helps retain moisture and improves product texture and absorption
It exfoliates dead skin cells
It kills acne-causing bacteria

question: What should you be aware of if the word fragrance or parfum appears on an ingredient list?
options: It simply means the product smells nice with no concerns
Fragrance or parfum is an umbrella term that can hide many undisclosed chemicals and is a common cause of skin irritation and allergies
Fragrance listed on a label is always natural
Fragrance only causes problems if it is listed first

question: What does mineral oil do in skincare and is it harmful?
options: Mineral oil is toxic and should never be applied to skin
Mineral oil is a safe effective occlusive ingredient that seals in moisture though some prefer to avoid it for personal reasons
Mineral oil clogs pores in all skin types always
Mineral oil is only used in prescription skincare

question: What is dimethicone on a skincare ingredient list?
options: A type of chemical exfoliant
A silicone-based ingredient that creates a smooth skin feel fills in fine lines temporarily and acts as a skin protectant
A type of preservative
A fragrance masking ingredient

question: What does the ingredient phenoxyethanol do in skincare?
options: It brightens skin tone
It is a common preservative used to prevent bacteria and mould growth in skincare products
It exfoliates and removes dead skin
It provides hydration to skin

question: What does an e-mark before a volume measurement on packaging indicate?
options: The product is eco-certified
It indicates the average fill quantity meaning the actual content may vary slightly but meets average regulations
The product was made in Europe
The product has been electronically tested

question: What does the ingredient zinc oxide indicate when found in a skincare product?
options: It is a chemical exfoliant for rough skin
It is a mineral sunscreen ingredient that physically blocks UV rays and is also soothing for irritated skin
It is a type of moisturising ingredient
It is an anti-ageing compound that boosts collagen

question: What does it mean when an ingredient appears near the very bottom of an ingredient list?
options: It is one of the most important ingredients
It is present in a very small concentration often below 1 percent
It is an ingredient that was added last minute
It is an ingredient that only activates when mixed with others

question: What does the ingredient caprylic or capric triglyceride mean on a label?
options: A type of fragrance oil
A lightweight emollient often derived from coconut oil that moisturises skin without feeling heavy or greasy
A chemical exfoliant
A type of sunscreen ingredient

question: What does free from on a skincare label generally mean?
options: The product is available for free
The product has been formulated without specific ingredients that the brand highlights usually as a marketing claim
The product contains only free range natural ingredients
The product is exempt from safety testing

question: What does the term ophthalmologist tested on eye cream packaging mean?
options: The product was approved for use inside the eye
An eye specialist has evaluated the product to check it is suitable and safe for use around the delicate eye area
The product was created by an ophthalmologist
The product treats eye conditions like dry eyes

question: What ingredient should people with an aspirin sensitivity be cautious of in skincare?
options: Hyaluronic acid
Glycerin
Salicylic acid which is related to aspirin and may cause reactions in people with aspirin sensitivity
Niacinamide

question: What does the ingredient lactic acid do and which category of exfoliant is it?
options: It hydrates and is a type of BHA
It is an AHA that gently exfoliates the skin surface making it smoother and is also hydrating
It is a type of retinol for anti-ageing
It is a fragrance-derived ingredient

question: What does the certification logo COSMOS or ECOCERT on packaging indicate?
options: The product has been to space
The product meets organic and natural cosmetics certification standards
The product is made in an eco-friendly factory
The product is approved by European doctors

question: What should you do before using a new skincare product regardless of what the label claims?
options: Use it all over your face immediately to test it fully
Do a patch test on a small area of skin first to check for any adverse reactions before applying to your full face
Read all online reviews before trying it
Only start using it if it is recommended by a celebrity

question: What does the ingredient polyglyceryl on a label indicate?
options: A type of strong acid exfoliant
An emulsifier and gentle surfactant often used in natural formulas as an alternative to more synthetic emulsifiers
A silicone-based smoothing ingredient
A type of retinol derivative

question: What does it mean when a label says tested under dermatological control?
options: A dermatologist created the product from scratch
The product was used by volunteers under dermatological supervision to monitor skin reactions and tolerability
The product is prescription only
The product was tested on synthetic skin in a lab

question: What is the best habit when it comes to reading skincare labels as a consumer?
options: Trust all marketing claims on the front of the packaging
Turn to the ingredient list on the back to understand what is actually in the product and learn to recognise key ingredients and potential irritants
Only buy products that have the longest ingredient list
Never use products with more than five ingredients
